summary: The NSF Awards API returns an unordered page and offers no amount sort, so any client-side --min-amount filter can only see the rows it fetched; a full page must warn the user that matching awards may lie beyond it.
reason: Unlike NIH RePORTER, which is sorted award_amount DESC and therefore guarantees unfetched results are smaller, NSF gives no such ordering. Without the warning a user running --min-amount believes they have seen every award above the threshold when they have only seen a subset of the first page. A regen that reintroduces a bare client-side filter, or that assumes NSF honours a sort parameter, silently restores the false-completeness bug.
